topics.title: Whale Watching in Mirissa
topics.description:
We all love spending our leisure time doing activities which could attract our minds and provide us with joy. Among thousands of these recreational activities, admiring the  beauty of nature, as well as the animals, are one of the most popular  and enjoyable activities preferred by thousands of people in the whole world. If you visit Srilanka, don't forget to reach Mirissa, a super destination in Srilanka for whale watching.

 If you are interested in Whale watching, studying and photographing the largest animals on the planet, Mirissa, a popular destination in Dondra point which is considered as one of the world's best whales and dolphin sighting her territories would be a perfect match for you.

topics.title: Best Time to Visit
topics.description: The best time period to watch whales at Mirissa is between November and April. Take back the fond memories of the gentle giants of the ocean; It is a lifetime experience. Mirissa is the most reliable place for whale watching in Sri Lanka. This is because whales are 90 per cent more likely to be seen at the right time and because of the well-developed infrastructure.

topics.title: Luxury Boat Experience
topics.description: Our modern whale watching boats are equipped with comfortable seating, breakfast, drinking water, toilet facilities, life jackets, insurance coverage and experienced crew members to ensure a safe and enjoyable journey.

topics.title: Marine Wildlife
topics.description: Besides Blue Whales, visitors often encounter Sperm Whales, Bryde's Whales, Spinner Dolphins, Sea Turtles, Flying Fish and occasionally Killer Whales during the tour.

topics.title: Getting to Mirissa
topics.description: Mirissa is located on Sri Lanka's beautiful southern coast, approximately 150 km from Colombo. Visitors can reach Mirissa by private transfer, expressway, train or bus. Tours Mirissa also provides reliable airport transfer services.

topics.title: Photography Tips
topics.description: Bring a zoom lens, sunglasses and keep your camera ready throughout the trip. Whale sightings can happen unexpectedly, so having your camera prepared will help capture incredible memories.

topics.title: What to Bring
topics.description: Wear comfortable clothing and bring sunscreen, sunglasses, a hat, camera and motion sickness tablets if required. We recommend carrying only essential belongings for maximum comfort.

topics.title: Frequently Asked Questions
topics.description: Find answers about bookings, cancellations, children, weather conditions, departure times and everything you need to know before joining your whale watching tour.
